Selle metoodika abil saab teadusküsimuste hüpoteese mõõta, määrata ja analüüsida.<br/><br/>Uuring hõlmas küsimustike kasutamist, mis olid eelnevalt ette valmistatud ja küsimused läbi vaadatud, et leida nende asjakohasus uuritavas kontseptsioonis. Küsimustike kasutatav keel oli suhteliselt mõõdukas, et inimesed sellest aru saaksid, ning menetlus oli arusaadav.<br/><br/>Populatsioon ja proovivõtumenetlus<br/><br/>Uuringus osales 20 meest-10 ja 10 naist erinevatest elukutsetest. Uuringus osales suur hulk osalejaid.<br/><br/>Instrumentatsioon<br/><br/>Uurimisvahendiks kasutati küsitlusküsimustikke, mis olid näost-näkku intervjuud. Näost-näkku intervjuus esitati kõigile osalejatele küsitlusküsimustikud, et määrata kindlaks nende tajutav tase, kuidas nad selgitavad oma seisukohti meedias, kui see kujundab Hollywoodi filmide reaalsust ja rolli, eriti naiselikkuse kujutiste kujundamisel. Küsimusi oli kahte tüüpi, avatud ja suletud, mis olid suunatud erinevatele ametitele.<br/><br/>Usaldusväärsus<br/><br/>Uuringu usaldusväärsuse säilitamiseks valiti osalejad nende erinevate ametite järgi. Küsimustikud olid suunatud erinevate elukutsete esindajatele vastavalt küsimusele.<br/><br/>Andmete kogumise kord<br/><br/>Küsimustikku kasutati põhjalikuks intervjuuks. Igale osalejale esitati 11 avatud ja 3 suletud küsimust. Iga küsimust selgitas teadlane, et tagada vastaja mõistmine ja mugav vastus. See uuring kestis pool tundi.<br/><br/>Esmased uurimistulemused<br/><br/>See doktoritöö tutvustab esmase uurimistöö tulemusi. Esmane uurimistöö viidi läbi küsimustike koostamisega ja need küsimustikud jagati 20 osaleja vahel. Osalejad jagunesid kahte rühma - 10 meest ja 10 naist. Uuring viidi läbi andmete kogumiseks, iga osaleja selgitas selgelt iga küsimust.<br/><br/>Intervjuu tulemused on toodud allpool<br/><br/>Küsides meessoost kehade kohta, mida meedia näitab, ütlesid mõned, et reklaamitööstus näitab sageli toonides, sihvakas, Brad Pitt-like-jocks. Kuid teised ütlesid, et meelelahutustööstus näitab õiglast osa pot-bellied meestest nagu Jack Black. Vastusena küsimusele, kas need pildid mõjutavad otseselt meeste arusaama endast. Mõned mehed ütlesid, et võib-olla see mõjutab noorte meeste arusaama, et nad peavad olema kuue-pack, kuid mõned mehed mainisid, et keskealiste meeste sõnum on see, et neil on suurepärane naissoost partner, olenemata sellest, milline on nende enda keha tüüp.<br/><br/>Küsides, milliseid naissoost kehasid meedia näitab, mõned naised ütlesid, et igas vanuses ja kogu meedias näivad olevat õhukesed ja väikesed. Ja mõned neist mainisid, et on erandeid, nagu Melissa McCarthy, kuid need erandid on endiselt vähe ja sageli piiratud komöödia žanriga. Vastus küsimusele, kas need pildid mõjutavad otseselt naiste arusaama endast, vastas mõni, et on täiesti tõsi, et naised peavad kogu oma elu jooksul jääma õhukeseks ja uhkeks, kuid mõned ütlesid, et nad on rahul oma isiksusega, mis neil on. Kui küsiti, kas selliseid pilte peetakse ühiskonnas ideaalseks, vastasid mõned naised, et see ei ole kindlasti olemas, sest need pildid püsivad ebarealistliku ja idealiseeritud taju, mis on viinud söömishäirete ja madala enesehinnangu epideemiani kogu noorte põlvkonnas kogu maailmas.<br/><br/>Nad mainisid ka, et sellised pildid ei ole realistlikud, kui vaadata inimesi mis tahes linnas maailmas, on selge, et sellised kehatüübid on erand, mitte norm. Kui küsiti, kas meedias on meeste ja naiste välimusest vale muljeid, vastasid mõned, et õhupuhastus peidab loomuliku naha tegelikku tekstuuri. Kaukaasia mudelite ülekaal saadab sõnumi "valge" ilu standardist. Fotode ostmine muudab ebaloomulikult tõeliste inimeste arvud androgüünideks, ilma et neil oleks liha.<br/><br/>Vastusena küsimusele, kas mõni film kujutab mehi kui peamist tegelast meeste tähtsusest meie ühiskonnas naiste ja laste suhtes, ütlesid mõned, et hiljuti avaldatud "Mälestusmärkide mehed" ülistab meeste rühma tegevust, kes vapralt kaitsesid kunsti aardeid natside poolt hävitatud. Kuid tõeline lugu oli mitmeid naisi, kes aitasid seda ohtlikku ülesannet. Kustutades kõik peale ühe neist naissoost tegelased, film jääb püsima maailmavaade, et naised on tarbetu meie ühiskonnas ja selle ajalugu. Vastusena küsimusele, kas filmis esinevat naist saab võrrelda tema rolliga "päris maailmas", mainisid mõned, et naised on sageli taustategelased enamikus filmides, enamasti kahemõõtmelised tegelased - naine, tüdruksõber, partner - kuid alati relatsioonis meestega oma elus.<br/><br/>Reaalses elus naised on karmim, targem, ja palju suurem roll elus oma perede ja ühiskonnas. Mõned ütlesid, et naiste tegelased filme on sageli stereotüübid – soppy, vamp, slutty, vaimukas, jne Naised reaalses elus on palju keerulisem ja sageli ühendada palju atribuute stereotüüpe, ja rohkem. Kui vastata küsimusele, kas naised filmides toimivad eeskujudena, siis mõned naised ütlesid, et naiste kujutamine filmides peegeldab nende sekundaarset positsiooni ühiskonnas. Kuigi nad on pool inimkonnast, tunnistab meedia neid ainult pooleks oma toodete "turust". Filmides ei ole naistele palju häid eeskujusid, hoolimata naiste hiljutisest kasvust, mis keskendub tugevate naiste tegelastega teemadele, nagu "näljamängud" või "külmutatud".<br/><br/>Arutelu<br/><br/>Uuring näitas, et pooled meestest ja naistest olid mõjutatud meediast oma kehakujunduse jaoks. Enamik neist vastas, et nad jälgisid neid "ideaalseid" "stereotüüpseid" mees- ja naissoost kehasid filmides. Mõned neist mainisid, et neile meeldib oma kodus tegutseda nagu nende lemmiknäitleja või näitleja.<br/><br/>Uuringu tulemus andis segatüüpi vastuseid, et meedias kujutatud pildid mõjutavad otseselt naiste taju enda kohta mõnikord ja naised peavad kogu oma elu jooksul jääma õhukeseks ja uhkeks, kuid mõned naised ei mõjutanud neid pilte. Mõned naised on vastuolus selle ideega, et selliseid pilte peetakse ühiskonnas ideaalseks. Nende sõnul püsivad need pildid ebarealistliku ja idealiseeritud taju.<br/><br/>Hollywoodi kino roll naiselikkuse kujutiste kujundamisel<br/><br/>Pärast maailmasõda keskendusid Hollywoodi filmid äärelinna elu esindamisele. Naiste kujutamine piirdus naissoost koduperenaistega, kes on magus ja alistuv ning jääb oma koju. Betty Friedani "Naiselik müstika" (1963) algatas arutelu keskklassi koduperenaiste sisemiste saladuste üle, kes elasid väsinud ja üksildast elu. See aitas kaasa soolisele arengule, mis ühiskonnas üldse toimus (Perruccio, n.d., lk 55). 1967. aastal ja hiljem hakkasid filmid seda murrangut kujutama koduperenaiste suhetes ja otsima ravi ja vaimsete häirete ees. Filmid kujutasid nende elus valitsevat segadust, kuid ei pakkunud mingit ravi (Perruccio, n.d.).<br/><br/>Naiste esindatus juhtivates rollides: Uuringud, mis viidi läbi naiste koha läbivaatamiseks, sealhulgas 50-aastaseks perioodiks, näitasid, et nad ei võtnud filmides ega peamise aja programmides olulist kohta. Jonesi (1942); Clarke (1969); Seggar et al (1981); Douglas (1995); ja nii paljud teised teadlased näitavad, et naiste meediaesitlus ei ole tõeline ja kujutab neid rohkem kooskõlas võrreldes nende tegeliku seisundiga ühiskonnas (nagu on tsiteeritud Eschholz et al, 2002). Need uuringud kinnitavad ka, et neile pakutakse ainult vähem olulisi ja stereotüüpseid rolle.<br/><br/>Eschholz et al. uuris 1996. aastal 50 parimat filmi, et uurida naiste ja vähemusmeeste esindatust ekraanil 1990. aastatel. Kuigi nii naised kui ka vähemused on ületanud paljusid traditsioonilisi piire tööjõu ja sotsiaalsete normide osas, on Hollywoodi filmitööstus endiselt peamiselt valgete meeste omanduses ja juhitud (Eschholz et al. 2002, lk 313). See näitas, et valge mees domineeris pidevalt ekraani esitluses. Naised, kes moodustavad 51% kogu elanikkonnast USA (USA Census Bureau 1990) esitati ainult 35% juhtivaid rolle, mis näitavad levimust traditsioonilise naiselikkust 1990. aastatel. Rassilise ja eetilise vähemuse esindatuse tõttu jäi samasugune alluvuse maskuliinsus. Aafrika-ameeriklased või hispaanlased mängisid ainult 20% juhttegelastest. Lisaks olid need peategelased peamiselt enamasti mitte-valge elanikkonnaga filmid. Filmid, mis hõlmavad tegevust ja seiklust, kujutasid mehi naiste üle.<br/><br/>Naiste vanuse esindatus Hollywoodi filmides: varasemad uuringud Hollywoodi filmides naiselikkuse ravi kohta näitavad, et naiste füüsiline välimus ja nende noorusliku atraktiivsuse kujutamine on väga oluline. Keskealistel ja vanematel naistel oli peaaegu välistatud rollid filmides. Meeste tegelaste kujutamine oli täiesti erinev, kuna neid prognoositi küpsemaks ja targemaks. See peegeldas Ameerika reklaamijate ja tarbijate stereotüüpe, mis tajuvad, et naised saavutavad noorusliku ilu ja võlu oma teismeliste ja kahekümnendate aastate jooksul.<br/><br/>Nende füüsiline atraktiivsus kaob koos vanusega ja ema rolli muutumisega, kuid mehed olid stereotüüpsed kui intelligentsed ja atraktiivsed vanusega ning säilitasid seksuaalsuse isegi isade ja vanaisadena (Wolf 1991; Faludi 1991; Douglas 1995, nagu tsiteeritud Eschholz et al, 2002). Hall (1995) ja Rhodes (1993) leidsid, et see diskrimineerimine oli lisatud stereotüüpide maskuliinne ja naiselik intellektuaalne võimekus liiga (nagu tsiteeritud Eschholz jt, 2002). Vähemusi kujutati peamiselt noorte, koomiline ja kriminaalne rollid. Baptista-Fernandez ja Greenberg (1980) avastasid, et valged ameeriklased olid küpsemad ja vanemad kui nende afroameeriklased (nagu on tsiteeritud Eschholz et al, 2002).<br/><br/>Need uuringud toetasid USA kodanikuõiguste komisjoni aruande (1977) järeldusi, et "valged mehed kipuvad olema vanemad ja küpsemad, samas kui naised ja vähemusrühmad on tõenäolisemalt nooremad ja vähem küpsed" (nagu tsiteeritud Eschholz et al, 2002, lk 306). Eschholz et al (2002) leidis, et meeste ja naiste vanusekriteeriumi diskrimineerimine jäi samaks ka 1990ndatel. Enamik naisi esitati alla 30 või nooremad, samas kui mehed esitati vanemad, küpsed ja isegi oma 50s.<br/><br/>Eelmistes uuringutes on patriarhaalne perekonnaüksus eeskujuks, kus naissoost tegelased peaksid kinni traditsioonilisest naiselikust rollist täita oma kohustusi naiste ja emadena. Nende karjääripüüdlusi ei peeta oluliseks. Vastupidi, mehed näitavad üles väga mehelikke omadusi, nagu õigus ja vabadus väljakutseid täita ja saavutada edu oma perekonnast eraldi. Emad ei tööta tõenäoliselt väljaspool kodu kui mehed, kes omistavad tähtsust abielule ja lapsevanematele (Eschholz, 2002).<br/><br/>Sugurollid tööturul ja ametialane prestiiž: see ilmneb Clarke'i (1969); Robertsi (1970/71); Long ja Simoni (1974); Northcutt et al (1975) uuringutes, et mehed domineerisid ekraanil ametialast valdkonda. Ekraanireaalsust võib nimetada valge mehe maailmaks isegi siis, kui feministlik liikumine oli laialt levinud (nagu on tsiteeritud Eschholz et al, 2002). Kuid Japp (1991); Graham ja Maschio (1995/96); Signorielli ja Bacue (1999) näitasid, et feministliku liikumise tõttu oli naiste ametialaste rollide kujutamine paranenud. Kvantiteet ja kvaliteet olid aga piiratud nii naiste oluliste kui ka prestiižsete ametikohtade puhul (nagu on viidatud Eschholz jt, 2002).<br/><br/>Vende Berg ja Streckfuss (1991) leidsid, et "67 protsenti tööga seotud tegevustest olid eksponeeritud meessoost tegelased. Tööhõive sektorid, kus naissoost tegelased olid sageli kaasatud jaemüügi ja teeninduse. Tööstuses ei olnud naisi ja mehed kalduvad olema kõrgematel organisatsioonilistel ametikohtadel (nagu on viidatud Eschholz et al, 2002). Eschholz et al (2002) leidis ka oma uuringust, mis hõlmas 50 1996. aasta filmi, et meessoost filmitegelased domineerisid selles valdkonnas. See uuring ei olnud aga seotud varasemate uuringutega, mis käsitlesid tööhõive staatust ja rassi vähemusi. Kuigi valimi ülevaatamine näitab, et enamik mittevalgeid ei olnud tööl kuni filmide valmimiseni. Kolm Aafrika-Ameerika naist filmis "Seadke see välja", kes töötasid alguses, kuid ei hoidnud seda kuni lõpuni, näitab seda suundumust.<br/><br/>Kui filmis "Esimeste naiste klubi" oli esindatud kolm valget naist, kes saavutasid filmi valmimise suunas prestiiži. Valgete naiste esindatus näidati rahaliselt sõltuvana nende meessoost kolleegidest. Tööalase prestiižiga seotud sooline erinevus selgus 1996. aastal tehtud 50 filmi uuringus, mis olid sel ajal sotsiaalsele reaalsusele väga lähedal. Kuid rassilised erinevused olid märgatavad, kusjuures filmides oli afroameeriklaste ametialase prestiižiga seotud palju väiksem prestiiž. Selle erinevuse projektsioon ekraanil oli suurem kui ühiskonna tegelik riik. Uuringus leiti, et naiste ja rassiliste vähemuste jaoks oli filmides töövabadus, kuid naiste domineerivates ametites oli meeste ilming piiratud naissoost domineerivates filmides (Eschholz et al, 2002, lk 319).<br/><br/>Sajad filmid, mis ilmusid aastatel 1946-1989, et uurida naiste rolli Hollywoodi filmides. Ta avastas, et meessoost naiste suhe ei näidanud muutusi naiste asemel, sest nad esindasid ainult kakskümmend viis protsenti kõigist nendes filmides. 1960. aastate feministlikule liikumisele järgnenud filme kujutati aga kui väljaspool kodu töötamist. Kuuskümmend üks protsenti filmides aastatel 1976-1989 kujutatud naistest olid näidatud mittetraditsioonilistel ametitel. Lisaks sellele näitab ta, et tänapäeva naist kujutatakse rohkem vabastatuna seksi algatamisel, seksuaalsuhetes väljaspool abielu ja vägivaldsetes tegevustes. Naiste kujutamine ei nõua neid tingimata abielus. See uuring näib olevat vastuolus enamiku uuringutega, mis leiavad mehelikku hegemooni nii töökohal kui ka seksuaalsuhetes (Rothman et al 1993, nagu tsiteeritud Eschholz et al, 2002).<br/><br/>Sugu: 20. sajandi kinodes tehtud uuringud mehelikkuse ja naiselikkuse projitseerimise kohta näitavad, et valged mehed on vägivaldsed ja agressiivsed. Nad on kavandatud probleemi lahendajatena ja võivad oma eesmärkide saavutamiseks minna mis tahes piiridesse. Vähemusmeestele pakutakse tavaliselt otsmikkinos koomiksirolle, et tahtmatult tähelepanu või halastamatute kurjategijatena osaleda (Hall, 1995; Rhodes, 1993, nagu on viidatud Eschholz et al, 2002).<br/><br/>Need uuringud näitavad "hegemooniliste maskuliinsuse" tunnuste levimust, mis on seotud valgete meestega televisioonis ja filmis. Nad domineerivad ekraanil esitatud piltide üle. Naised on ekraanil traditsioonilise naiselikkuse poole kaldunud ja vähemusmeeste esindatus filmides peegeldab alluvat mehelikkust. Need on esitatud stereotüüpsetes rollides, millel puudub tähtsus tööjõu ja õigussüsteemis. 1996. aasta filmides soo ja rassilise vähemuse esindatuse uurimisel leiti, et meessoost tegelased olid rohkem kalduvus traditsioonilise mehelikkuse poole ja naissoost tegelased olid rohkem kalduvus traditsioonilise naiselikkuse ja romantilise naiselikkuse poole.<br/><br/>Kuid mõned erandid olid olemas, näiteks "Jerry, mida mängis Tom Cruise Jerry Maguire'is, ületas keskmise traditsioonilise mehelikkuse, intellektuaalse mehelikkuse, traditsioonilise naiselikkuse ja romantilise naiselikkuse" (Eschholz et al, 2002, lk 320). 1996. aasta Hollywoodi filmides näitasid ka mõned naissoost tegelased üles kasvavat mehelikkust ja naiselikkust. "Reene (Renee Russo) Tip Cup, Karen (Sally Fields) in Eye for a Eye, Tally (Michelle Pleiffer) in Up Close ja isiklik, Stoney (Jada pinkett) in Set It Off, ja Brandi (Lynn Whitfield) in Thin Line vahel Armastus ja vihkamine.32. etholz 2002 intensiivne tase nii mehelikkus ja naiselikkus"<br/><br/>Üldiselt läbisid need tegelased soolisi piiranguid, kuna neil oli oma elus suur kogemus. Jerry suurenenud tundlikkus (Jerry Maguire), kui tema karjäär oli liinil ja Kareni (Silma silma eest) suurenenud mehelikkus tema tütre vägistamise tulemusena on näited nendest sootunnuste muutustest. Manipuleerivad naiselikkuse tunnused (pettus, ebausaldusväärsus ja manipuleerimine) olid meestel rohkem nähtavad kui positiivsemad naiselikud tunnused, nagu traditsiooniline naiselikkus ja romantiline naiselikkus. Need leiud viitasid varasemate uuringute tulemuste täpsusele. Kuid erandina näitasid valged mehed manipuleeriva naiselikkuse kujutamisel rohkem punkte kui valged naised (Eschholz et al. 2002).<br/><br/>21. sajandi Ameerika kino on arenenud aktiivseks jõuks soolise suhte tajumisel. Sajandi alguses ei näinud tootjad ja avalikkus meedias naiste kuvandit kui sotsiaalse ebavõrdsuse representatsiooni. Järk-järgult kriitikud ja publik muutunud rohkem mures rolli filme mängida määravaid sotsiaalseid väärtusi, institutsioonid ja väljavaated. Ameerika kirjanikud nagu Molly Haskell ja Marjorie Rosen koos Joan Melleniga hakkasid 1970ndate keskel näitama agressiooni soolise rolli ja naiste seksuaalsuse alandavate ideoloogiate vastu klassikalises Hollywoodi kinos. Haskell uskus, et klassikalised Hollywoodi filmid keskendusid rohkem "mehe hinge ja pääste" kujutamisele. Naiste tegelastel oli filmides peaaegu mingit välimust ja tähendust. Nad töötasid võrdluspunktina ja näitusena meessoost juhtivatele rollidele, kellel ei olnud ambitsioone ega arvamust. Nad kujutasid endast ohtu mehelikule reaalsusele ja seetõttu passiivseks, mitte-interaktiivseks ja patriarhaalsesse sotsiaalsesse süsteemi sukeldunud (Wardak, 2013).<br/><br/>Hollywoodi uus laine oli periood 1960. aastate keskpaigast kuni 1980. aastate alguseni.<br/><br/>Et mõista Hollywoodi filmide rolli naiselikkuse kujundamisel tegelikkuses, peame hoolikalt uurima mõningaid populaarseid filme, mis põhinevad tüüpilisel naiselikul teemal ja nende mõjul ühiskonna naiselikkuse kujutise kujundamisel.<br/><br/>Bonnie ja Clyde (1967)<br/><br/>See on näide uuest feminismi lainest, mis toimus Hollywoodis sel ajal. See film kujutab Bonnie Parkerit kui idealistlikku naisjuhti, kes erineb üsna naiste, emade või seksiobjektide traditsioonilistest rollidest. Bonnie iseloom kujutab naise vastupanu domineerivas meesühiskonnas. Film keskendub naiste vabastamisele ühiskondlikest normidest ning on Hollywoodi New Wave’i filmis üks eriline teema.<br/><br/>Clyde Barrow (Warren Beatty) on pangarööviga seotud jõugu juht ning Bonnie Parker (Faye Dunaway) on röövis oma armuke ja kaasosaline.<br/><br/>Keset seksuaalse revolutsiooni tippu jõudes on naised seksuaalse soojuse ja vabadusega. Püüdes elada vabastatud elu, kasvab ta lõpuks seksisümbolina ja saavutab kuulsuse kuritegevuse kaudu. Filmi avapilt näitab, et ta nälgib millegi üle, mida tal pole.<br/><br/>Film kujutab tema iseloomu mitmemõõtmelisel viisil, muutes teda oma seksuaalsusega tugevamaks. Soov muuta oma elu ettekandjana, muutub ta enesekindlaks naiseks, kellel on relv ja muutub kuriteo kaasosaliseks ja tema armastusega Clyde. Tema seksuaalne soov jääb Clyde'i impotentsuse tõttu täitmata ja ajab teda pettunud. Ebatavaliste feministlike tunnuste näitamine suunab tema pöördumatu seksuaalse oomphi kriminaalsesse rütmi ja kasutab oma loomingulist energiat luuletuses "Bonnie ja Clyde lugu". Bonnie ja Clyde’i feminism tõstatab feministlikke küsimusi.<br/><br/>Filmis on kujutatud naiste seksuaalsuse ja soorollide muutumisega seotud küsimusi.<br/><br/>Lunstad, oma artiklis "Aga teil ei oleks Gumption seda kasutada": Bonnie ja Clyde ja seksuaalrevolutsioon, uurib filmis esindatud feminismi aluseks olevat teemat. Ta väidab, et Bonnie rahulolematus temaga rõhutas režissöör alguses. Penn ise kirjeldab seda avamisjärjestust kui - tema näljaste huulte lähivõtteid ... see on see, mis see on - nälg rohkem kui tema praegune olemasolu. "<br/><br/>Kaamera fookus nihkub tema näole ja siis alasti keha rõhutab tema pettumust tavapärase naiseliku rolliga. Edasi, kujutades tema nägu baaride vahel, teeb ettepaneku, et ta peab ennast oma olemasolevas olukorras vangistatud. Seejärel "ta täielik korpus aknaraamis ei viita mitte ainult sellele, et tema vangistus tuleneb tema lõastatud soovist, vaid et ta on ka metatekstuaalselt raamitud kui kuuekümnendate vabanenud naise inconographic representatsioon" (Lunstad, n.d., lk 15). Lunstad tunneb, et Bonnie iseloom, läbi oma seksuaalsuse, on näide naise kaasasündinud tundest ennast rahuldada ühiskondlikus kontekstis, mis ei kiida seda heaks. Ta väidab, et "pilt temast ekraanil ei mängi meeste fantaasiaid naise keha kohta, vaid pigem tema kui täielikult seksuaalse naise olemasolu naiste liikumise kontekstis kinnitab naiste õigust seksuaalsele soovile" (Lunstad, nr.d, lk.17). Bonnie iseloomu, tõeliselt esindab ja mõjutab ühiskondlike normide valitsevad ajal feministliku liikumise, kirg ja olemus, samuti seksuaalselt rahuldust näitaja.<br/><br/>Alice ei ela enam siin (1974)<br/><br/>See film kujutab naiste võitlust sotsiaalsete normide piirangutest vabanemise eest. Alice (Ellen Burstyn) on töölisklassist pärit kohusetundlik koduperenaine, kelle elu eesmärk on rahuldada oma abikaasat ja kasvatada oma sõnakuulmatut poega. Ta soovib saavutada vabanemist kodukohast ja saada iseseisvaks. Lugu peegeldab feministlikke võrdõiguslikkuse teemasid kõigis era- ja avaliku elu valdkondades, mis valitsesid sellel perioodil (1960. ja 1970. aastatel). Alice on kujutatud 35-aastase koduperenaisena veokijuhina. Pärast abikaasa surma soovib ta laulda (enne abiellumist) oma kodulinnas.<br/><br/>Filmis on kujutatud Alice’i teekonda üksikemana, samuti naisena, kellel on unistused, mida täita, ja ambitsioonid, mis on edukad. Tema suhtumine iseseisvusesse esindab Ameerika naiste püüdlusi võrdsuse ja vabaduste eest soorollides. Alice Hyatt oli 1970. aastatel ekraan, kui üksikemadust peeti tabuks. Reisi ajal saab ta teadlikuks, et ta ei saa veeta oma elu kellegagi, kes kohtleks teda kui alistuv ja alaväärsuslik ning lõpuks kasvab võrdse romantiline partnerlus Davidiga. Kesk- ja töölisklassi Ameerika naised sel ajahetkel võitlesid sarnaste probleemidega. Nad identifitseerisid Alice'i iseloomu, kes võitles elatise eest ilma ametliku koolituse ja hariduseta. Nad nägid feminismi kui väljapääsu patriarhaalse süsteemi ühiskondlikest piirangutest.<br/><br/>Stepfordi naised (2004)<br/><br/>Filmi "Stepford Wives" (Forbes, 1975) remake oli ka filmi "Stepford Wives" (Stepford Wives) remake. Filmi kaudu püüti uurida feministlikke küsimusi, nagu näiteks naise seksuaalne identiteet, mis on seotud Ameerika ühiskonnas valitseva patriarhaalse süsteemiga. See kujutas äärelinna naiste rahulolematust koduse hüppega (Peruccio, n.d.).<br/><br/>Film räägib Joanna ja tema pere nihkest Stepfordi linna, kes järk-järgult jõuab linna saladuseni, et selle naissoost elanikud on robotid. Filmi aluseks olevad teemad on maskuliinsus, naiselikkus ja soolised võimusuhted, mis esindavad mehelikku hegemooniat ja naiste vabaduse rõhumist ning nende ambitsioonide mahasurumist. Filmis kujutatakse stereotüüpseid naisrolle, mis esindavad traditsioonilist naiselikkust, nagu seda täheldatakse ühiskonnas üldiselt. Naised on kujutatud igapäevaselt majapidamistöid, nagu toiduvalmistamine, koristamine ja muidugi rahuldada oma abikaasa vajadustele iga üks viis.<br/><br/>Neil ei ole palju tarkust, vähemalt ei ületa oma abikaasade intelligentsust, ja nad on kaugel kõigist töökohtadest, mis nõuavad intellekti ja võivad majandusele kaasa aidata. Film projitseerib naisi ideaalsete koduperenaistena patriarhaalsetes ühiskondades, kes ei ole ambitsioonikad ja kellel ei ole oma arvamusi. Nende absoluutne kuulekus oma abikaasadele on traditsioonilise naiselikkuse ideaalsete tunnuste ilming. Tähelepanuväärne on ka nende ajude mikrokiibide sümboolne tähendus. Seega on nad oma abikaasade absoluutse kontrolli all, kes on määratud jääma kodumaisesse sfääri, teenides oma abikaasasid pühendunult ja alistunult.<br/><br/>Maskuliinsuse osas on film projitseerinud mehi täielikus traditsioonilises maskuliinsuses. Neid esindavad stereotüübid, mis iseloomustavad tõelisi mehi nende saavutuste poolest võrreldes naistega. Film on hea näide patriarhaalsetest ühiskondadest, mis toovad kaasa nii meeste kui ka naiste puudused.<br/><br/>Mona Lisa naeratus (2003)<br/><br/>Film ei esinda siiski traditsioonilisemat perioodi, mis ei kiitnud heaks naiste sotsiaalseid väljavaateid, keskendudes peamiselt kodumaisele ja teatud määral alluvusele. See kujutab peamiselt teravat konflikti kahe vastandliku hoiaku vahel, mida esindavad liberaalne feminist Katherine Watson ja tavapärane Betty Warren. Feministlik perspektiiv on esitatud üsna põhilise, ainsusena, nagu seda näeb Watsoni vähendav ja liiga ühemõõtmeline vaade naistest, kes on piiratud kodumaiste rollidega. Soolise võitluse keerukust minimeeritakse, kui film liigub Johni (Julia Stiles) valiku järgi jääda koju laste ja perega, mitte töötada Yale'i õigusteaduskonnas. See näide on näide vaatajatele, et feminismil võib olla erinev tõlgendus erinevatele inimestele. Naiste valikut olla oma pere ja lastega lähedane ja nende eest hoolitseda tuleks valesti tõlgendada kui stereotüüpse soorolli klammerdumist.<br/><br/>Need tegelased on tõelised naised, kellel on oma vead, võitlused ja nõrkused. Nad võitlevad oma elu muutmiseks ja vabanevad patriarhaalse sotsiaalse süsteemi seatud piirangutest. Nad võitlevad füüsiliselt ja emotsionaalselt oma iseseisvuse nimel.<br/><br/>Järeldus<br/><br/>Meedia mängib olulist rolli inimeste arusaamade kujundamisel oma identiteedi kohta ühiskonnas, kus nad elavad. Seega toimib meedia meie sotsiaalse tegelikkuse tähistamisel. Meestel on olnud domineerivad ametialased rollid televisioonis ja naisi on sageli näidatud stereotüüpsetes traditsioonilistes naisrollides, nagu head naised, hoolitsevad emad, pruudid ja serverid. Naiste esindatus väikesel ekraanil on ikka sama, mis see püsis umbes 10 aastat tagasi. Reklaamid mängivad olulist rolli meeste ja naiste idealiseeritud, rassistlike ja seksistlike piltide edastamisel. Reklaamijatel ei ole mingit seletust, miks nad kujutavad moonutatud naiselikkust meedia kaudu. See on tõsi<br/><br/>Meedia toetas väljajätmisi ja stereotüüpe, mis viisid vähem võimu omavate sooliste ja rassiliste vähemuste ekspluateeriva ja rõhuva kohtlemiseni ühiskonnas. Naiste ja vähemuste ebarealistlik kujutamine meedias on vastutav selle eest, et tarbijad võtavad omaks rassi ja sooga seotud silmakirjalikkuse. Hoolimata sellest, et nad on pool inimkonnast, tunnustab meedia neid ainult poole oma toodete "turust". Filmides ei ole naistele palju häid eeskujusid. Feministliku teemaga filmide ülevaade näitab, et vaatamata nende keskendumisele naiste vabastamisele ühiskondlikest normidest, on aluseks olev teema naiste rahulolematus oma passiivse ja alistuva rolliga patriarhaalses sotsiaalsüsteemis.<br/><br/>Annoteeritud bibliograafia<br/><br/>Raamatud<br/><br/>Käesoleva raamatu autorid keskenduvad peamiselt sotsioloogilisest vaatepunktist klassi, soo, rassilise etnilise päritolu, vanuse, geograafilise elukoha jms teemadele. Sotsioloogia aluspõhimõtetest annab tervikliku ja teoreetiliselt tasakaalustatud ülevaate illustratiivsete uuringute ja andmete kasutamine ülalnimetatud sotsioloogiliste aspektide ja paljude teiste aspektide suhtes. Autorid arutavad meedia rolli ebarealistliku naiselikkuse kujutamisel erinevate meediakanalite ja eriti reklaamide kaudu. Raamat tutvustab ka sotsioloogi Melissa Milkie katset uurida kultuuriväravahoidjate rolli meedias feminiinsuse ebarealistliku kujutamise määratlemisel ja säilitamisel.<br/><br/>Autorid selles artiklis püüavad paljastada, kuidas vahendatud suhtlemine USA mõjutab sotsiaalset struktuuri rassi ja soo. Nad ka arutada, kuidas need vahendatud side mõjutab meie arusaam rassi ja soo ühiskonnas.<br/><br/>See peatükk räägib meedia ja kultuuriga seotud põhimõistetest, mis kordavad sotsiaalteaduslikke ja sotsiaalseid ehituspunkte. Pärast erinevate meediaallikate arutamist arutab see peatükk edasi meedia mõju.<br/><br/>Raamat näitab, kuidas meedia mõjutab reaalsuse sotsiaalset ülesehitust, annab meediatöö põhitõed, teooria ja ajaloo ning arutleb, kuidas meediainimesed tegelevad postmodernsete muutustega. Samuti arutatakse kultuuri väravavalvurite rolli moonutatud pildiga naiste esitlemisel meedias. Lisaks sellele, viidates Melissa Milkie tööle "Vaidlustatud naiselikkuse pilt: kultuuri väravavalvurite võitluste analüüs "Tõelise tüdruku" kriitikaga", paljastavad need meediaisikud, kes vastutavad nende piltide loomise eest.<br/><br/>Avalike suhete areng (n.d.)<br/><br/>See peatükk annab ülevaate avalike suhete tavade tähtsusest iidsetest aegadest. See heidab valgust avalike suhete meetodite jätkuvale arengule, mida kasutatakse alates iidsetest aegadest kuni tänapäevani. Lisaks uurib see silmapaistvate peategelaste panust avalike suhete valdkonnas.<br/><br/>Artiklid<br/><br/>Artiklis käsitletakse soo mõistet psühholoogilistes mõistetes. Autor Sandra Bem illustreerib traditsioonilise mehelikkuse ja traditsioonilise naiselikkuse tunnuseid, mis on leitud erinevate inimeste käitumises. Ta selgitab, et tugevalt meheliku ja tugevalt naiseliku käitumise vahel on võimalik säilitada tasakaal, et erinevates oludes korralikult toimida. Ta avastab teise liigituse soo selles osas nimetatakse androgüün (andro viitab mees ja gyn viitab naissoost).<br/><br/>Selles artiklis uuritakse, kuidas Hollywoodi filmid projitseerivad naisi ja vähemusi, liigitades inimesi paljudesse erinevatesse kategooriatesse. Artiklis on tabel, mis näitab, et autor on teinud uuringu, mis näitab Hollywoodi ühiskonna erinevaid kategooriaid, mis jagab näitlejad sõltuvalt nende kategooriast erinevateks rollideks. Näiteks on filmides autori sõnul erinevat tüüpi naiselikkust, traditsioonilist naiselikkust, mis tavaliselt hõlmab vanamoelisi naisi, kes on alati seotud pere ja laste ideega. See artikkel on väga kasulik ja on väga oluline vastata küsimusele. Lisaks on käesolevas artiklis tehtud uuringud kasulikud, sest see keskendub Hollywoodi ühiskonnale ja uuringu viimane osa keskendub Hollywoodi filmidele.<br/><br/>Artiklis käsitletakse hiljutist arengut meeste välimuse kujutamisel meeste hooldustoodete reklaamides Suurbritannias. Viidates 2000ndate aastate keskel Ühendkuningriigis televisioonis esitatud Lynxi seeria reklaamidele, püüab autor neid tekste uurida, pidades silmas nende vastavust või väljakutset hegemoonilisele mehelikkusele.<br/><br/>Lunstad, J.K. (n.d.) Aga sul ei oleks seda kasutada: Bonnie ja Clyde ja seksuaalrevolutsioon<br/><br/>Artiklis analüüsitakse gangsteri- ja feminismifilme, eriti Bonnie ja Clyde'i (1967) žanrifilme. Autor esitab feministliku arusaama Bonnie iseloomust, kes ei kõhkle oma seksuaalsuse paljastamisest. Autor tunneb, et tema tegelane kinnitab naiste õigust väljendada oma seksuaalset soovi.<br/><br/>Artiklis on esitatud esitlus äärelinnaelu kujutamisest ajal, mil feministlik liikumine oli oma tipus. Autor arutleb soorollide jaotuse ja inimeste arusaamade üle teemast, esindades selle perioodi filmides mehelikkust ja naiselikkust. Ta arutleb äärelinna koduperenaiste frustratsiooni ja õnnetuse kujutamise üle nende positsiooniga ühiskonnas. Artiklis on ka nende meeste mõtteviis, kes olid rahaga seotud ja ei soovinud välja tulla oma traditsiooniliselt ette nähtud mehe rollist.<br/><br/>Artiklis tõlgendatakse hegemoonilise naiselikkuse mõistet seoses Connelli (1995, 1987, 1995) välja töötatud mitme mehelikkuse ja hegemoonilise mehelikkuse mõistega. Autor pakub tõlgendust hegemoonilisest mehelikkusest ja hegemoonilisest naiselikkusest ning erinevatest toonidest igas ja ka nende seostest ja ristmikest klassi, rassi ja etniliste erinevuste valguses.<br/><br/>Artikkel esitab analüütilise vaate Hollywoodi filmides naiste tegelaste kujutamisele teise laine feminismi ajal. See uurib, kuidas peavoolu kino tegeleb feminismiga, kasutades näiteid mõnest seitsmekümnendate keskel tehtud Hollywoodi filmist feministliku teemaga.<br/><br/>Filmid<br/><br/>Film uurib 1950ndatel Ameerikas toimunud sotsiaalseid arenguid ning kujutab traditsiooniliste ja liberaalsete hoiakute võitlust soolise diskrimineerimise vastu ühiskonnas. Film pakub ka mitmekülgset naiselikkuse tõlgendust erinevatest vaatenurkadest.<br/><br/>1975. aasta originaalfilmi "The Stepford Wives" uusversioon on selge pilt tüüpilisest patriarhaalsest ühiskonnast, kus naised on piiratud igapäevaste töödega, lisaks hoolitsevad oma abikaasade nõudmiste eest. Neid kujutatakse õrna, lahke, pehme ja ilusate objektidena, kellel ei ole ambitsioone ja isiklikku arvamust. Mehed on näidatud tüüpiliste traditsiooniliste mehelike tegelastena, kes on võimsad ja ei lase oma naistel neid ületada. Nad lähevad ulatuses muutes neid robotid rahuldada oma mees ego. Joanna iseloomu mässab vastu traditsioonilise naiselikkust on sunnitud naised. See film on täiuslik näide esindatuse naiselikkust Hollywoodi filme.<br/><br/>Bonnie ja Clyde vabastatakse keset seksuaalse revolutsiooni tippu jõudmist. Woma n seksuaalse soojuse ja vabadusega. Oma püüdlustes elada vabastatud elu kasvab ta lõpuks seksisümboliks ja saavutab kuulsuse kuritegevuse kaudu.<br/><br/>Lugu peegeldab feministlikke võrdõiguslikkuse teemasid era- ja avaliku elu kõigis aspektides, mis valitsesid sellel perioodil (1960. ja 1970. aastatel). Film kujutab Alice'i teekonda üksikemana, kes on jäetud üksi, et kasvatada oma poega ilma igasuguse kutsehariduse ja piisava hariduseta. Film kujutab ka naise unistusi olla sõltumatu ja ambitsioone edu saavutamiseks. Tema suhtumine iseseisvusesse esindab Ameerika naiste püüdlusi võrdsuse ja vabaduste eest soorollides.<br/><br/>Liide<br/><br/>Küsimustik<br/><br/>Tüüp A - avatud lõpetatud<br/><br/>Milliseid kehasid meedias näidatakse?<br/><br/>Kas te arvate, et need pildid mõjutavad otseselt meeste arusaama iseendast ja kuidas mehed enda eest hoolitsevad? Palun selgitage lühidalt.<br/><br/>Milliseid kehasid meedias näidatakse?<br/><br/>Kas te arvate, et need pildid mõjutavad otseselt naiste arusaama endast ja sellest, kuidas naised enda eest hoolitsevad? Palun selgitage lühidalt.<br/><br/>Kas neid ideid peetakse meie ühiskonnas ideaalideks?<br/><br/>Kui palju inimesi on teil olnud, kellel on sellised kehatüübid nagu need, mida meedias näidatakse?<br/><br/>Millised valed muljed on meedias levinud meeste ja naiste välimuse kohta?<br/><br/>Kui paljud teie lemmikfilmid on mehed peategelasena? Milliseid sõnumeid nad soovitavad meeste tähtsuse kohta meie ühiskonnas naiste ja laste suhtes?<br/><br/>Loetelu mõned rollid naine on mänginud filme? Kuidas nad võrrelda oma rollid "päris" maailmas? Kuidas nad mõjutavad teie ootusi, kuidas naised tegelikult käituvad?<br/><br/>Kuidas on naiste omadused filmides võrreldavad naiste omadega oma elus? Kuidas need erinevad?<br/><br/>Mõeldes nendele filmi naistele, kuidas nad teevad teid (kui olete naine) teiste naiste suhtes? Kui hästi naised filmides toimivad eeskujudena, prillide vaatamisena või inimestena, kellega seostada?<br/><br/>B- tüüp - suletud<br/><br/>Kus te näete neid "ideaalseid" "stereotüüpseid" mees- ja naissoost kehasid kõige rohkem meedias? Ajakirjad? Filmid? Telesaated? Mis tüüpi telesaated?<br/><br/>Kas sa oled vahel oma lemmik näitleja või näitleja?<br/><br/>See uurimustöö meedia rolli kohta reaalsuse sotsiaalses ehituses |
| When you're looking to increase muscle size and build strength, you should focus on using more compound strength exercises. Research over time has demonstrated compound exercises are superior compared to other types of exercise. In fact, a 2017 study published in Frontiers in Physiology looked at exercise subjects who used compound versus isolation exercises over an eight-week period. The study showed that the group who focused on compound strength exercises had greater gains in both strength and VO2 max. A second study published in 2019, also supports the use of multi-joint (MJ) over single-joint (SJ) exercises when looking to improve strength in this case, in the lower body. Researchers reported significant strength increases in both SJ and MJ groups, but the MJ group saw significantly greater increases in 1-RM for all leg exercises that were tested in the study.<br/><br/>What Are Compound Strength Exercises?<br/><br/>Compound exercises are multi-joint movements that work several muscles or muscle groups at one time (ACSM). An example would be a Barbell Squat which works many muscle groups like the core, legs, hips and back. Another example would be a Bench Press exercise which works the muscles that make up the chest, shoulders and arms. Compound strength exercises are a staple in many exercise programs because they are ideal for building strength and adding size. In addition, a compound exercise will recruit more muscle fiber and in turn burn more calories per minute than a single-joint or isolation exercise. Compound exercises can be performed using body weight, exercise bands, dumbbells or your best option a barbell. This is because the average gym-goer can lift 20% more weight using a barbell compared to dumbbells. Compound exercise are also important because they mimic activities of daily living (ADL's).<br/><br/>An example of a compound (or multi-joint) exercise: Pull-up<br/><br/>Examples of Compound & Isolation Type Exercises<br/><br/>Compound (Multi-joint) Exercises<br/><br/>Isolation (Single-joint) Exercises<br/><br/>Squat<br/><br/>Leg Extension<br/><br/>Deadlift<br/><br/>Leg Curl<br/><br/>Dips<br/><br/>Tricep Extension<br/><br/>Military Press<br/><br/>Dumbbell Side Lateral Raise<br/><br/>Pull-Ups<br/><br/>Bicep Curl<br/><br/>Bench Press<br/><br/>Dumbbell Chest Fly<br/><br/>What are Isolation Strength Exercises?<br/><br/>Isolation exercises work only one muscle or muscle group and only one joint at a time (ACSM). Examples of isolation exercises include the Biceps Curl or a Leg Extension exercise.<br/><br/>Combining both mult-joint barbell and single-joint dumbbell exercises in a workout has been shown to work well. This type of combination can be seen in the new Jefit program, Compound Strength Routine. Many machine-based strength training products are designed with isolation exercises in mind. Some research has shown, however, that an isolation or single-joint exercise, like a biceps curl, can increase muscle hypertrophy more than a multi-joint exercise.<br/><br/>An example of an isolation (or single-joint) exercise: Bicep Curl<br/><br/>Jefit's New Compound Strength Routine<br/><br/>A new advanced strength program designed around multi-joint exercises is the Jefit Compound Strength Routine. The 3-day, advanced, strength training program includes 9-10 strength exercises in each workout. The routine offers three different strength programs, using barbell and dumbbells, and includes 1-3 supersets in each exercise session. This type of program design makes for a faster workout and in turn keeps all the session times less than an hour.<br/><br/>To wrap things up, please read this great list of guidelines from strength expert, Charles Poliquin, that discusses the pro/con of using both compound and isolation type exercises. It's definitely worth a read. Be well and stay strong!<br/><br/>We all know that exercise is like medicine for the body. There are times though when you just want to move through your workout in minimal time. This where a good strength and cardio circuit comes into play. This is why circuit training is so efficient, it combines the perfect blend of strength and cardio in minimal time. The end result is usually the same, a great full-body workout and a body covered in sweat!<br/><br/>The cool thing about circuit programs are: they're fast, fun and very effective at getting results. You never get bored because there are so many design options for a circuit routine. The featured strength & cardio circuit can now be found on the Jefit app.<br/><br/>What is Circuit Training?<br/><br/>Circuit training is training method that alternates between several exercises (usually 4 to 12) that target different muscle groups. A plan can even use different movement patterns (like push, pull, press, carry, etc.). The design of the program enables someone to move from exercise to exercise with minimal or no rest depending on their fitness level. Some of the many benefits of regular circuit training include improvements in body composition, muscular strength/endurance and aerobic capacity.<br/><br/>What is Interval Training?<br/><br/>Interval training, on the other hand, alternates between periods of moderate-to-high-intensity work with brief periods of active or passive rest. The main difference between circuit training and interval training is not what you're doing but rather the intensity of the work being done. You have probably heard about HIIT (high-intensity interval training) before or seen it in some of the workout titles on the Jefit app.<br/><br/>Equipment Need: Jump Rope, Weight Plate and Dumbbells<br/><br/>Circuit Design: UB/LB/Core/Cardio Sequence<br/><br/>The circuit session begins with four exercises group together following a brief warm-up. The session design alternates between an upper body exercise, a lower body exercise, and a core exercise before transitioning to cardio for 1-2 minutes. The program features 20 exercises (including four for warm-up) that comprise four rounds of "mini" circuits with four exercises in each group.<br/><br/>Here is what each of the four circuits (after the warm-up) look like in this latest Jefit circuit workout:<br/><br/>I have personally been doing circuits for many years now and, like a lot of people, working through one never seems to gets old. In any event, circuits are fun and get great results, so why not give one a try for your next workout?<br/><br/>The majority of people who exercise or engage in individual or team sports often looks for ways to improve performance. With that, brings us to how we can better "hack" our body to improve performance, some also call this DIY science….biohacking. Dave Asprey, a biohacker who created the company Bulletproof, defines biohacking as "the art and science of changing the environment around you and inside you so that you have full control over your own biology."<br/><br/>Why Try to Hack Your Body Anyway?<br/><br/>There are many people out there who try to hack their body to improve performance, on some level. They do this basically because they have a strong desire to feel better and to see just how far they can push the human body. A lot of people are hacking their body essentially to try and live as long as possible. Dave Asprey as an example, has been quoted as saying he wants to live to 180 years old.<br/><br/>Another well-known body or bio hacker is Tim Ferris, author of the best-selling book, The 4-Hour Body. Ferris has a well known reputation for trying to hack just about everything related to his body. Why does he do it? This Wired interview explains why.<br/><br/>Now that you have a better understanding of what trying to hack your body is all about, check this out.<br/><br/>Mindful breath work effects everything from mediation to sports performance.<br/><br/>Breath Work: An Easy Way to Improve Performance<br/><br/>We all know how to breath intuitively and how importance breathing is since it gives us life. Go beyond this for a moment and listen to this great Wild Ideas podcast from REI. The podcast, comes out every other Monday, and just featured author, James Nestor, author a new book, Breath: The New Science of a Lost Art. The book comes out in May. The podcast talked about his years of doing research and talking to medical experts on the science of breathing. He offers up some great easy-to-follow tips that you can use right now. I actually tried his 6-second breath technique on my morning walk today. You can try this when seated (or like me, walking). Take in a long, slow breath through your nose only, for 5-6 seconds. Then exhale slowly for the same amount of time and try this for about 6 repetitions. The goal of this type of breathing, is to help more nitric oxide enter your body and tissues. It's been reported that when you breathe through your nose, nasal resistance increases by 200% and this helps the release of oxygen. If you were wondering, mouth breathing does not let your body take advantage of the sinuses production of nitric oxide.<br/><br/>Nasal Versus Mouth Breathing<br/><br/>Try closing your mouth and just breath slowly in/out through your nose for about minute. According to a lot of the science out there, "breathing through your nose is one of the most beneficial things you can do for the overall health of your body and for your longevity." This is what Nestor talks about in his book and in the podcast. You may already know the value of breath work, if you practice yoga on a regular basis. Think about this for a minute. How great would it be if we could get a legitimate boost in performance by simply breathing slowly through our nose? Listen to the podcast and give it a shot. For additional reading, check this great article out on the science of breathing by Sarah Novotny and Len Kravitz, Ph.D. and this research paper on effects of nasal breathing in runners.<br/><br/>There are many experts and researchers who think breath work should become a component in health & fitness model. Meaning, you work on strength, flexibility, cardio, nutrition, etc. – why not also incorporate breath work as part of your daily routine?<br/><br/>Mobility: Unlock Tight Hips to Improve Performance<br/><br/>We typically spend a great deal of our time in the gym pushing weights or doing cardio. One key area that often gets overlooked is mobility. Mobility can be defined as freedom of movement without pain through a full range of motion. Mobility exercises can be done as a warm-up if you're always rushed for time. They are great for reducing joint pain, improving a fuller range of motion and can even reduce the chance of injury. We all know tight muscles and connective tissue are an accident waiting to happen.<br/><br/>When you want to squat, lunge, or lift weights better, mobility work is key, especially when it comes to the hips. You may have limited hip mobility because of an old injury, you don't work on mobility or you may sit or drive all day for work. In any event, tight hips can cause, over time, a chain reaction resulting in dysfunctional movement. Over time your hip joints will become tight if not addressed appropriately, you'll begin to notice issues when performing exercises like Squats and Deadlifts.<br/><br/>What are Some of the Better Hip Exercises to do?<br/><br/>There are a lot of different directions you could go here. This is an opportunity to use the Jefit app and perform this series of exercises. Complete each exercise below slowly, working through a full range of motion. Perform each exercise as a hip and glute warm-up prior to working out and you'll eventually see an improvement in hip mobility. Some may not be pure hip mobility drills but doing these will in turn improve glute/hip function. Perform each exercise for 30-seconds then move to the next and repeat the circuit twice.<br/><br/>No one needs to tell us that we're currently living in unprecedented times. The health of everyone in this country, and worldwide for that matter, is at the forefront of all our minds. It is more important than ever to attempt to follow a healthy lifestyle. How do you know if you're living a healthy lifestyle in the first place? Harvard Health reports you're considered healthy if you can answer "yes" to all the following criteria. (1) healthy diet, (2) healthy body weight, (3) never smoked, (4) consume moderate amounts of alcohol and (5) exercise regularly.<br/><br/>What's Considered a Healthy Lifestyle?<br/><br/>According to Harvard Health, one important component to this type of lifestyle is a healthy diet. Meaning, an "intake of healthy foods like vegetables, fruits, nuts, whole grains, healthy fats, and omega-3 fatty acids". In addition, avoid unhealthy foods like "processed meats, sugar-sweetened beverages, trans fat, and sodium." No smoking in a no-brainer. A healthy body weight according to the site, is a body mass index (BMI), between 18.5 and 24.9. But to be honest, this is not the best metric to monitor, instead focus on your percent body fat. On the alcohol side, no more than one drink/day/women and two drinks/day/men. A healthy physical activity level means roughly 30-minutes of moderate to vigorous activity most days of the week.<br/><br/>Does Living a Healthy Lifestyle Actually Add Years to Your Life?<br/><br/>The research does in fact demonstrate that living a healthy lifestyle can add years to your life. Individuals who met the criteria for all five habits (listed above) enjoyed living longer lives than those who had none: 14 years for women and 12 years for men to be exact. People who had none of these habits "were far more likely to die prematurely from cancer or cardiovascular disease." There is also additional research that reports similar findings to this in the Journal of American Medical Association.<br/><br/>Are you getting a minimum of 30-minutes of moderate or vigorous activity most days of the week?<br/><br/>You probably have the exercise piece down already, especially if you're using the Jefit app to help record and track your workouts. Here are some additional ways to move towards a healthy lifestyle, in addition to the five criteria mentioned in the research studies above.<br/><br/>10 Ways to Help You Live Better and Longer<br/><br/>Exercise<br/><br/>Burn 1,100 Calories a Week. Duke University scientists discovered that this amount of calories expended from exercise prevents the accumulation of visceral adipose tissue (fat). This type of belly fat causes arterial inflammation and hypertension. Are you falling short of this number? Try joining a sports a league. One study reported that people who exercised in groups boosted their average weekly calorie burn by 500 a week.<br/><br/>Hit the Weights. University of Michigan scientists found that people who completed three strength workouts/week for two months lowered their diastolic blood pressure by an average of eight points. That's enough to reduce the risk of stroke by 40% and heart attack by 15%.<br/><br/>Find the Time to Exercise. People who exercise for 2 hours/week are less likely to feel stressed than their sedentary counterparts, say researchers from Denmark.<br/><br/>Get on Those Daily Chores. Doing 150 calories' worth of chores a day can lower blood pressure by 13 points, according to Medicine & Science in Sports & Exercise. The reduction lasts only 8 hours, but make it a daily habit and you can lower your blood pressure in the long term.<br/><br/>Diet & Nutrition<br/><br/>Drink Five 8-Ounce Glasses of Water a Day. Those drinking this amount of H2O were 54% less likely to suffer a fatal heart attack compared to people who drank two glasses a day.<br/><br/>Try a Natural Remedy. Israeli scientists found eating one grapefruit a day lowers cholesterol by 20% even in people who don't respond to statins.<br/><br/>Cut Down on Sweets. Tufts University researchers found low-sugar diets had lower levels of depression and anxiety than those who consumed all types of carbohydrates. The happier people also limited their total carbohydrate intake to 40% of their daily total calories.<br/><br/>Indulge Your Chocolate Craving. A 15-year study by Dutch scientists found men who ate 4 grams of cocoa/day had half the risk of dying from heart disease than those who ate less. That's the equivalent of two 25-calorie Hershey Kisses – an amount that can fit into any diet.<br/><br/>Hopefully this article has offered you a little more insight on what constitutes a healthy lifestyle. If so, maybe you feel like you're more equipped now to live a more healthy, sustainable lifestyle. Continue to focus on improving your mind body & spirit a bit more each day. Be Well and Stay Strong!<br/><br/>The benefits of strength training performed on a regularly basis are well documented in scientific literature, magazines and on the web. There are many digital health & fitness companies who have apps that enable you to build strength training programs. With so much information coming your way, what type of program is actually best at helping you get results? In this case, when talking about results, we're referring to gains in both strength and muscle development.<br/><br/>Take a look at the following 5×5 split routine found on the Jefit app. This particular weight lifting program was designed as a 3-day routine. Keep in mind, there are many other split routines you can find that offer 4-6 days versus 3-days.<br/><br/>Program Design: 5×5 Split Routine (3-Days)<br/><br/>All strength training sessions follow a 5×5 format using only two body parts to keep session times under an hour. The workout time range for the 3-day program was between 36 and 56 minutes. The recovery time between sets is a very important training variable that needs to be manipulated depending on load (sets x reps x weight). Adequate recovery is important in order to push that next heavy set. A key point to remember, using a short rest period of one-minute between sets means the muscle is only about 80% recovered. I used a 2:00 recovery time between most of the sets for this reason. That may have to increase if someone is using very heavy weight for all their exercises.<br/><br/>The routine gets its unique name from "splitting" up specific muscle groups and associating those body segments to different days of the week. The idea behind the design of this routine was to couple a leg day with pulling movements that overload the back on Day 1. The second day includes push movements that target the chest with a pull and push for the arms. On day 3 you have pressing movements that target the shoulders with a few core exercises. This routine is only a snapshot for one-week of training.<br/><br/>The 5×5 program used the following 3-day split format over the course of a week:<br/><br/>Legs & Back (4 exercises) – Day 1<br/><br/>Chest & Arms (4 exercises) – Day 2<br/><br/>Shoulders & Core (5 exercises) – Day 3<br/><br/>Sets & Reps Scheme<br/><br/>Be realistic when designing any exercise program regarding the number of sets and repetition you use. More is not always better. Different exercises, sets, repetitions and recovery time will effect both short and long-term outcomes. Using a 5×5 setup gives you 25 repetitions per exercise and two movements per body part brings that repetition total to 50. That is more than enough to overload a muscle using a 5-RM. Many programs out there, when looking at sets and repetitions, equate to unrealistic expectations regarding length of workout. A repetition goal of 25-50 is more than enough to stimulate muscle growth, as long as you're using a 5-RM. Here is a nice article on how to perform a 5-RM bench press test.<br/><br/>There are four important design elements regarding this particular 5×5 split routine. They are: (1) the use of compound movements, (2) large muscle groups, (3) the use of 5-RM on all exercises, and (4) sufficient recovery time. A 5×5 split routine is popular and has been shown to build strength and muscle size over time. Special emphasis should be placed on your 5-RM in this strength training routine. During anytime in the program, if you're able to surpass five repetitions for any exercise – that's right – you need to increase the weight. If for example, an exercise on your "core" day (see below) is too light – then hold a weight plate or wear a weighted vest (if available) to challenge yourself more. See the design and layout below.<br/><br/>I hope you enjoy the program. If you have any questions on this particular 5×5 Split Routine (3-day), now featured on Jefit app, or any other program for that matter, please reach out to me in the comment section on this blog or our online community via the app. Here is some additional reading that you may find interesting on the topic of strength training. Be well and stay strong!<br/><br/>There is nothing more upsetting than not getting results after dedicating yourself to dieting and exercise over the course of a few months! You pushed the weights regularly, did cardio and improved your diet, but in the end, there was still that unwanted layer of body fat covering your abs. It may have been because you did not focus enough on controlling one very important item…added sugar.<br/><br/>Are Doing Sit-ups Enough?<br/><br/>The answer to that question is NO. A study done in 1984 at the University of Massachusetts looked at various fitness outcomes of subjects who performed 5,000 sit-ups over the course of a month. Performing hundreds of sit-ups on a daily basis wasn't enough to lose abdominal fat. The subjects, a group of college students, had body measurements taken as well as a painful muscle biopsy procedure. The subjects body fat didn't change and not even an inch was lost around the abdominal area by the end of the study. In the end, they had much stronger abs but their body fat and girth remained unchanged.<br/><br/>Many factors can influence the way you look and feel on a daily basis as well as over the course of your lifetime. A healthy, sustainable lifestyle also plays a huge part in how lean you ultimately get. You have probably heard that genetics are also important. True. Don't forget about physical activity (in and out of the gym), this plays a significant role too. The missing "ingredient" in most exercise plans though is cutting back and monitoring added sugar.<br/><br/>What is Your DASI? Daily Added Sugar Intake<br/><br/>The term, DASI, is an acronym that I coined and stands for daily added sugar intake. It's an important component of any nutrition program and it's a game changer for those looking to get ripped. For the majority of people, getting a lean, ripped mid-section will be a lifelong challenge. Some never seem to realize that how they fuel their body in turn effects their midsection and abdominal area. This goes well beyond doing a daily plank challenge. Learn from the story of the UMass college students.<br/><br/>Follow These 3-Steps to Get Strong, Ripped Abs<br/><br/>Beware of added sugar in all foods and drinks. How? Start reading food labels and keep track of your daily added sugar. Put yourself on a sugar budget. Eat no more than 150 calories of added sugar a day for men. That's about 38 grams a day for men and 100 calories or 25 grams a day for women. Carbohydrates (sugar) contain 4 calories per gram. There are two types of sugars, natural sugar and added sugar. Added sugar is hidden in just about everything we eat and drink. Examples of natural sugar are milk and fruit, and unlike added sugar, they contain more fiber, vitamins and minerals. Added sugar has minimal nutrients, basically no fiber, and can quickly raise blood sugar levels like all fast food or junk food.<br/><br/>Add a weekly HIIT session on the cardio side, in addition to your weekly strength training sessions. Do this by adding intervals into a cardio session with bouts of hard work followed by brief periods of recovery and repeat several times. A whole cardio session could be an interval-based workout for 15-20 minutes or you can periodically add it to the cardio work you're doing now. Any type of cardio will do the trick from jogging to rowing.<br/><br/>1-RM Test. Push your strength training workouts. Use one day to determine a new 1-RM (one repetition maximum) involving all your major exercises, like bench press, shoulder press and squat and do a reset on your current program. This can be done every 12-weeks.<br/><br/>Changing things up from what you had been doing is a great way to stimulate not only your body but also your mind. Finally, incorporating the three tips above into your new routine, will definitely move you in the right direction of getting the long wanted ripped abs. Use the Jefit app to help track your progress and keep you moving toward your goals. Remember, you don't own it until you right down or record it, so use the app. Good luck Stay Strong!<br/><br/>It seems like every time we pick up a magazine or surf the web we're overwhelmed with outlandish weight-loss claims. Let's do a reset on this for 2020 and change the narrative. Rather than focus on weight loss like so many of us do, let's start looking more at our percent body fat level. Do you know what your current body fat level is? You should know this number and monitor it over time.<br/><br/>The ideal body fat percentage for an adult varies depending on the age of the individual. Other variables that also come into play are gender, genetics, bone structure and their exercise level. College-age men typically carry 15% body fat while women have 23%, keep in mind that these numbers are for non-athletes.<br/><br/>Stepping onto a bathroom scale does not tell you the real story about your overall health. Your body weight is not as important as how much body fat you're carrying. Once you can determine your body fat level, you then have a better understanding of the ratio of muscle to fat that you have.<br/><br/>For example, a women who weights 145 pounds and 33% body fat, can calculate that she has 48 pound of fat and 97 pounds of muscle, bone and fluid. A male, who is 205 pounds and has 25% body fat can determine he is carrying 51 pounds of fat weight and about 154 pounds of muscle, bone and fluid. Once this is known, you can start using the Jefit app to keep track of how this number changes over time. In both of these cases, the goal would be to lose fat weight while maintaining or gaining muscle, depending of course what the goals are.<br/><br/>Monitoring your body fat is important, and in turn, offers great insight into the status of your overall health & fitness. As you see, it's a valuable metric to follow and offers insight into understanding if a particular strength training program is actually working.<br/><br/>The at-home workout program has unexpectedly become the new norm due to area gyms closing as a result of COVID-19. A by-product of this is that we are spending more of our time inside. Many are now wondering how we can take our old workout plan we did at the gym and incorporate it into a new home routine?<br/><br/>Now that we are home-based, there seems to be additional questions we need to think about and work around. In addition to the question above, there are other matters to worry about too, like when you're going to exercise, space availability, and equipment needs.<br/><br/>Approaching Your At-Home Workout Program Differently<br/><br/>A paradigm shift is a "a fundamental change in approach or underlying assumptions." This may be a perfect time to start thinking differently about how we exercise at home, meaning, how we structure and execute our training routine. Change can be a very good thing when it comes to an individuals body and how that body adapts and progresses during a training cycle.<br/><br/>5 Basic Human Movement Patterns<br/><br/>Whether your training plan is geared towards a full-body, split routine or circuit-training, should not be the focus. What really matters most is training movements not muscles. Do not rely on specific exercises, instead, make sure you add-in specific movement patterns during each training session. Movement patterns are exercise classifications and can be thought of as the movement direction of the exercise. There are different schools of thought on how these movement patterns are categorized and even talked about.<br/><br/>The following is a modified version of some of the basic human movement patterns that should be included in the design of any training program. This list could also include other categories like hip dominant, knee dominant, rotational and anti-rotational categories. For the purposes of this article though, we will refer to the following five basic categories.<br/><br/>Squat<br/><br/>Any variation of a Squat is considered one of the best compound movements you can do. Exercises listed in this category are considered hip or knee-dominant by some. You can think of this category as exercises that utilize muscles around the hip and knee, like a Barbell Squat, Dumbbell Squat, or a Goblet Squat, as examples.<br/><br/>Hip Hinge<br/><br/>This particular category of exercises consists of movements that involve a "hinging" motion at the hip joint, and have little to no movement at the knee. Classic examples of exercises that incorporate a hip hinge are a Kettlebell Swing and a Romanian Deadlift.<br/><br/>Hip Hinge Category: Kettlebell Swing exercise<br/><br/>Pull: Vertical & Horizontal<br/><br/>The vertical pull includes moving a weight or body weight vertically, relative to the position that your body is in. Examples of these types exercises would include Pull-ups and Lat Pull-down.<br/><br/>The horizontal pull include any exercise that moves the weight toward your body horizontally. A few examples include: Bent-over Rows, Inverted Row or a Seated Row.<br/><br/>Push: Vertical & Horizontal<br/><br/>This category combines both vertical and horizontal in order to make life easier for you. The vertical push, includes exercises that move a load or weight vertically in relation to the torso, like a Military Press or Push Press.<br/><br/>Horizontal push is a category of exercises that involves moving a weight straight out in front of you, away from the body, like a Bench Press.<br/><br/>Weighted Carry<br/><br/>Many strength and conditioning experts agree that a Weighted Carry is the best, all-in-one exercise a person can do. The Carry is ideal for increasing overall strength, especially back, core and grip strength. The exercise benefits don't stop there though; it's also ideal for improving stamina and functionality. A simple definition of a Weighted Carry is picking up a weight or a load and carrying it for distance or time. The best exercise example is a Farmer's Carry.<br/><br/>The importance of adding these five movement patterns into your at-home workout program is invaluable. It offers someone a better way to design and customize their training program to meet all their needs. It also assures that an individual will work through the various planes of motion more often during training compared to a traditional training plan. The benefits of focusing on movement patterns instead of working specific muscles ensures a well-balanced, strong, functional body.<br/><br/>Exercise Program Design<br/><br/>Now that you have a better understanding of movement patterns, it's important to use them in your next at-home workout. To experience measurable gains with your at-home training program, it's important that you understand the basic concept of periodization.<br/><br/>"Periodization is an organized approach to training that involves progressive cycling of various aspects of a training program during a specific period of time."Len Kravitz, PhD<br/><br/>To get the most benefit out of any at-home workout program, a periodized training plan should be followed. This is where adjusting the various training variables over time (i.e. sets/reps/rest/load/time under tension) comes into play. The idea is to control these variables during each training day and over the course of a full training year. By following such a training format over time, you'll ensure maximal gains long-term, safely and effectively. Think about that for a moment. If you have not had strength gains in a while, maybe a lack of periodization is the culprit?<br/><br/>It seems like the whole world is now moving life indoors due to COVID-19. The new playground for families and individuals has transitioned from outdoors to inside the home. You can take advantage of this opportunity in the coming months by taking back control of your health and fitness. We may not be able to control certain outcomes regarding the coronavirus, but what we can control, is our overall health and wellbeing during this stressful time. One of the first steps down this path is making the commitment to work out regularly at home.<br/><br/>Make Working Out at Home a Priority<br/><br/>Exercise for most people is never a priority. There is now plenty of time to workout even with the kids stuck inside and you working remotely. The average adult still spends too much of their time sedentary, according to research from theJournal of the American Medical Association. Those numbers are going to potentially increase with COVID-19 lingering for the coming months. The goal is to find a time to exercise that works best for you and your schedule; maybe it's in the morning, after work, or small bouts throughout the day. Check out the following suggestions to make your workout from home a good overall experience:<br/><br/>Schedule your workout each day as an appointment and put into into your calendar or smartphone and set a daily reminder.<br/><br/>Morning workouts are a great way to start the day off right and it puts you in a great mood once you're done by releasing endorphins.<br/><br/>Turn your workout routine into a habit by staying on track by "just "doing it" for the next 3-weeks. Daily exercise should eventually become part of your lifestyle like eating or brushing your teeth. It needs to become part of your regular day.<br/><br/>Make it social (make it a family affair, join your spouse or get the kids involved if need be).<br/><br/>Do a periodic self-assessment to track progress and keep you motivated. Keep track of particular strength gains, body composition changes, etc. You can test yourself, for example, every 4, 8, or 12-weeks and document it using the Jefit app.<br/><br/>Home Equipment Options<br/><br/>There are many different roads someone could travel down when looking at options to meet all their exercise needs. There are now a variety of virtual classes on everything from yoga to HIT classes but many include costly monthly subscriptions. We have taken all the guess work out of deciding what you need in terms of getting started. The following article, published on the Jefit blog, offers the best exercise equipment options on a budget.<br/><br/>Your Assistant Coach: the Jefit App<br/><br/>The JeFit app makes thousands of strength training routines accessible. It comes with a customizable workout planner, an extensive exercise library, and a members-only Facebook group page. You can choose new workouts and track your progress with the app so that you can see how close you are to your fitness goals.<br/><br/>Don't forget to join the Jefit community so that you can be a part of it too!<br/><br/>We are currently living in very turbulent times, and now more than ever, the benefits of choosing the best home gym equipment goes beyond increasing just strength. We are hearing, thinking, and reading more and more about the growing COVID-19 outbreak. It looks like living with the coronavirus will be the new norm for a while and it's enough to raise anyone's stress level. A continued, elevated stress level, coupled with no exercise could potentially suppress your immune system.<br/><br/>Benefits of Having a Home Gym<br/><br/>One of the best and safest options you have for getting in great shape is doing it right from the comfort of your own home or apartment. Not to mention, this seems to be a no-brainer with COVID-19 hanging around for months to come. In addition to the many health & safety benefits, there is also an associated cost savings resulting from your gym temporarily closing.<br/><br/>Cost Savings Benefit<br/><br/>You do not have to spend a ton of money setting up your home gym. There are many different pieces of equipment you could use but it will depend first on budget and then on availability of space and of course training experience. Let's assume that space, budget and experience are somewhat limited.<br/><br/>Exercise Equipment Needs<br/><br/>The following lists include multiple options for exercise products that are ideal for home use and can be purchased online. Each list offers versatile equipment, and is practical in terms of staying within a budget.<br/><br/>You can take care of your equipment needs while the Jefit team can help in terms of finding the best strength-training routines to help you get started.<br/><br/>Home Gym Option #1<br/><br/>Stability Ball<br/><br/>This product reaches far beyond just sitting on it for basic core work. Try lying in a supine position with your feet on the ball. Engage your core and raise your buttock off the floor, pause, and return to your starting position. Following a set of hip extension, perform the same movement with your feet on the ball and knees bent. Lift your buttock off the floor and hold in a bridge position, slowly lower and repeat. After completing these two movements, start in the same position but this time pull both feet in towards your buttock, performing a leg curl movement.<br/><br/>Foam Roller<br/><br/>If you're not rolling out on a regular basis, then you should honestly start. There are many benefits of adding in a few minutes of foam rolling before and even after your exercise session. Maintaining healthy fascia should be top of mind for you. Remember, restriction (i.e., connective/muscle tissue) is associated with disfunction and this can lead to a limited range of motion; and yes, this will eventually effect your performance.<br/><br/>Exercise Bands<br/><br/>Regular band work can be ideal for any exercise level. Bands are great to utilize in circuit-type exercise routines or as part of your dynamic warm-up. Different colored bands typically equate with different intensity levels. These are very inexpensive to purchase and can be brought anywhere for an exercise session.<br/><br/>Medicine Ball<br/><br/>This is another great tool for your exercise toolbox, if used correctly. It's ideal for adding resistance to various moves like squats, lunges or a step-up with a shoulder press. In the hands of the right person, a medicine ball can be a welcome addition to any home gym.<br/><br/>Jump Rope<br/><br/>One of the best forms of cardio exercise you can do is jumping rope. It's great for elevating your heart rate and easy to transition to – so it's ideal for any circuit-type or HIIT training program at home. Below is an example of how the JeFit app can help you keep track of this and many other exercises that you'll now be doing from home.<br/><br/>Home Gym Option #2<br/><br/>Adjustable Bench<br/><br/>This is a versatile piece of equipment for any gym. This multi-purpose bench will allow you to do many different upper body exercises and can even double as a "plyo-box" for steps-ups etc.<br/><br/>Dumbbells<br/><br/>A must for any home gym. Dumbbells, take up minimal space. A good pair of dumbbells can be used for just about any upper/lower body strength exercise. Start by purchasing a few pairs of dumbbells and build up to a full set over time.<br/><br/>Kettlebells<br/><br/>One of the many great things about this product is its wide scope of usage. You can use kettlebells for basic, traditional exercises to more advanced movements like a Carry exercise or a Turkish Get-up.<br/><br/>Ab-Roller<br/><br/>Another unique product that really targets your core and is great for developing core strength. It's one of those exercises that can really overload your core area so start slow and gradually build your repetitions and/or hold time.<br/><br/>TRX System<br/><br/>This training-system has become well-known and a staple used in everything from boot camp classes to home gyms. One of the best exercises you can do, in my opinion is an inverted row. If you ever want to test your strength level try completing 10 repetitions using strict form with a full range of motion.<br/><br/>Ever get to the gym, but find that you're just too tired or not bothered to actually work out? It's a completely normal feeling and there could be several reasons behind it. Here, we list why you may be feeling this way and recommend some solution to try and get your gym motivation back.<br/><br/>How to Get Your Gym Motivation Back<br/><br/>1. Get some sleep<br/><br/>Sleep deprivation can actually affect us much more than people think. If you've had a bad night or nights, then it can really impact your performance, as well as your mindset. Feeling tired and suffering from lack of sleep will really demotivate you.<br/><br/>Try to set a bedtime routine that you stick to. Go to sleep 7-9 hours before you have to wake up so that you can ensure you get enough sleep. Even if you may struggle at first, eventually, your body clock will remember and you'll be able to follow your sleep schedule.<br/><br/>2. Take a few days off<br/><br/>Yes, spending too much time at the gym can be a potential reason why you've lost motivation. When you train, you're constantly putting your body under stress. This is why rest days are important—this is when your body recovers. If you don't give yourself rest days, then your body doesn't have the time to recover properly. Sooner or later, you'll start to feel it.<br/><br/>So if you get to the gym and don't feel like working out, then take some time off. A day or two, or even more will help you take the time you need to recuperate. See how you feel after taking some gym time off.<br/><br/>3. Change your routine<br/><br/>Most of us tend to keep to the same workouts that we know. We stick to what is familiar and what we know works. Eventually, we become bored. If you're feeling unmotivated to go the gym, it could be because your fitness routine has become stale and stagnant. Doing the same workout over and over again will no longer challenge you. Without stimulation, we lose our motivation.<br/><br/>Try mixing up your training. Jefit has plenty of exercises that you can choose from to make a new workout plan and keep things exciting. Putting together something new and renew your motivation and get your gym mojo back.<br/><br/>If you want a change from the gym, then you can even try heading outside for a run or do some bodyweight exercises in the park. Join a new fitness class that you haven't tried before like boxing or cycling. A change of scenery might do you some good as well.<br/><br/>4. Define your goal<br/><br/>One of the biggest mistakes that people make is not having a fitness goal. Whether it's about losing weight, gaining muscle, hitting a new squat PR, or being able to run a half marathon in a certain time, having something to work towards makes the biggest difference in the gym.<br/><br/>5. Track your progress<br/><br/>Another reason why you may be feeling burnt out for the gym is that you don't see the progress you're making. If you're not tracking your progress, recording your workouts, taking progress pictures, then you won't be able to see how far you've come. It can be easy to fall into the rut of thinking that you haven't made a difference or that things have changed. This is why it's important to keep track.<br/><br/>Nothing can renew your motivation to get back into the gym than seeing where you were before and where you are now. It can help you picture where you will be in the future. Use a journal or a workout app (like Jefit!) that makes it easy for you to log everything and help get your gym motivation back.<br/><br/>6. Make it a habit<br/><br/>This may sound counterintuitive but you need more than motivation. Motivation is temporary and as you probably know, it comes and goes. To make sure that you keep going to the gym, even when you have no motivation, you need to make it a habit.<br/><br/>Set a fitness routine that you stick to, whether it's going to the gym before work or after work, or even during your lunch breaks. It can be difficult to start but if you keep it up, over time, it will become an integral part of your routine. Then you'll find yourself going to the gym even if you don't want to—because it's a habit. It's a great way to get your gym motivation back.<br/><br/>While you can try your best to avoid injuries, unfortunately, most people will experience them once in a while. If you are injured, then it's best to see a physical therapist or professional that can properly treat it. However, there are some common gym injuries, and here are some solutions to treat them.<br/><br/>How to Treat These Common Gym Injuries<br/><br/>1. Muscle Pulls and Strains<br/><br/>Other common gym injuries include pulling or straining your muscle. This is when a muscle is torn or overstretched.
